name: IEC 60909 — Prospective Fault Current Cascade
formula_reference: IEC 60909-0:2016 (short-circuit currents in three-phase AC systems), IEC 60865-1 (peak factor), BS 7671:2018+A3 Reg 434, NEC 110.9 + 240.86
implementation_note: Computes the prospective three-phase symmetrical short-circuit current (Ik") at each node in a cascade by iteratively adding source impedance + each upstream feeder impedance. Requires complex-number arithmetic (R + jX), per-stage transformer + cable + busbar impedance tables, and the IEC 60909 voltage-factor 'c' (1.05 max-fault, 0.95 min-fault). Also derives the peak factor n = 1.02 + 0.98 × exp(-3 R/X) per IEC 60909 / 60865. Inline LLM math gets the impedance addition AND the peak factor wrong on >30% of representative cases — must be tool-executed. Output feeds db-layout selectivity, breaker breaking-capacity check, and ADS disconnection-time check.

inputs.description: Nominal line-to-line voltage (400 TPN, 230 single-phase, 240/120 split, 480 US TPN)
Source type — drives source impedance modelling
Transformer kVA OR generator kVA OR UPS kVA
Per-unit impedance of source (transformer Zpu typically 4-6%, generator subtransient X"d typically 12-20%, UPS bypass typically 1-2%)
X/R ratio at the source — drives peak factor n
IEC 60909 c-factor — 1.05 for max fault current (Ik"max), 0.95 for min fault (Ik"min). Default max for breaker breaking-capacity check.
JSON array of stages: [{stage_id, fed_from, length_m, csa_mm2_or_awg, conductor_material, insulation, installation_method}]. Each stage adds R+jX in series. Empty array = fault at source terminals.
50 Hz (IEC) or 60 Hz (US) — affects cable X per IEC 60909-2 Annex B

ifault_ka_per_node.description: Map of {stage_id → Ik" RMS in kA} for every node in the cascade path

ipk_ka_per_node.description: Map of {stage_id → ip peak in kA} computed via n × √2 × Ik"

x_over_r_per_node.description: Map of {stage_id → X/R ratio at that node}

z_total_ohm_per_node.description: Map of {stage_id → |Z_total| in ohms} for reference

calc_basis.description: Either 'far-from-generator' (constant source) or 'near-generator' (with decrement) per IEC 60909-0 §3

runtime_contract.response: { ok: true, result: { ifault_ka_per_node, ipk_ka_per_node, x_over_r_per_node, z_total_ohm_per_node, voltage_factor_c_applied, calc_basis }, error?: string }
